2606.16682.title: Multimodal Evaluator Preference Collapse: Cross-Modal Contagion in Self-Evolving Agents
2606.16682.paper_summary: When AI agents use language models to evaluate their own outputs in a feedback loop, systematic biases emerge. We show that Evaluator Preference Collapse (EPC) is dramatically amplified in multimodal settings. Using GPT-4o to evaluate DeepSeek-chat across text and visual tasks, we find that a single strategy (step_by_step) absorbs 48.4% of all weight -- 3.2x the collapse observed in text-only self-evaluation -- while three visual-domain strategies receive only 9.1% combined weight. We then demonstrate a novel phenomenon we term cross-modal contagion: evaluator preferences acquired on one modality transfer to and corrupt strategy selection on another. Through a four-phase isolation training paradigm, we measure contagion coefficients and document strategy inversion -- the optimal strategy for a modality reverses after cross-modal exposure. A Phase 3 statistical validation across four evaluator configurations (N=53 total independent repetitions, 15,592 API calls) reveals a clear hierarchy: cross-model evaluation (GPT-4o, N=8) produces strong but symmetric bidirectional contagion (mean gamma_{T->V}=1.176, gamma_{V->T}=1.089, Delta=-0.088, p=0.575, Cohen's d=0.29); high round counts (DashScope, 50 rounds) cause collapse to single-strategy dominance (70% zero contagion); and self-evaluation provides near-complete immunity -- 97% of runs (N=30, DeepSeek-chat) yield exactly zero contagion (mean gamma=0.033, 95% CI [-0.031, 0.010], p=0.642, d=0.07). No evaluator condition shows statistically significant directional asymmetry. We introduce the contagion matrix indexed by evaluator identity, release the MM-EPC experimental framework, and identify cross-model evaluator architecture as the primary risk factor for preference contagion.
2606.16682.paper_summary_zh: 當人工智慧代理使用語言模型來評估其自身輸出時，系統性偏見會出現。<lb/>我們顯示，在多模態環境中，評估者偏好崩潰（Evaluator Preference Collapse, EPC）會被顯著放大。<lb/>使用 GPT-4o 對 DeepSeek-chat 在文本和視覺任務中的表現進行評估，我們發現單一策略（step_by_step）吸收了 48.4% 的所有權重——是文本自我評估中觀察到的崩潰的 3.2 倍——而三種視覺領域策略的總權重僅為 9.1%。<lb/>接著，我們展示了一種新現象，我們稱之為跨模態傳染（cross-modal contagion）：在一種模態上獲得的評估者偏好會轉移到另一種模態並腐蝕策略選擇。<lb/>通過四階段的隔離訓練範式，我們測量傳染係數並記錄策略反轉——一種模態的最佳策略在跨模態暴露後會逆轉。<lb/>在四種評估者配置（N=53 次獨立重複，15,592 次 API 呼叫）中的第三階段統計驗證顯示出明確的層級結構：跨模型評估（GPT-4o, N=8）產生強而對稱的雙向傳染（平均 gamma_{T->V}=1.176, gamma_{V->T}=1.089, Delta=-0.088, p=0.575, Cohen's d=0.29）；高回合數（DashScope, 50 回合）導致單一策略主導的崩潰（70% 零傳染）；而自我評估幾乎提供了完全的免疫——97% 的運行（N=30, DeepSeek-chat）產生了完全零的傳染（平均 gamma=0.033, 95% CI [-0.031, 0.010], p=0.642, d=0.07）。<lb/>沒有任何評估者條件顯示出統計上顯著的方向性不對稱。我們引入了以評估者身份為索引的傳染矩陣，發布了 MM-EPC 實驗框架，並確定跨模型評估者架構是偏好傳染的主要風險因素。
2606.16682.author: Zewen Liu et.al.
